scenario: A team wants a curated public API around a subset of decisions -- invoke from BPMN, expose only certain decisions, hide internal helper decisions, validate inputs. In DMN-1.3 this is the Decision Service construct. They want to know how to model it in Camunda 8.
question: Does Camunda 8 implement the DMN Decision Service element?

options.text: 3 specification pattern that Camunda 8's modeller and Zeebe DMN engine do not provide. md:23-28, the four supported DMN element types are Decision, Input data, Knowledge source, and Business knowledge model. Decision Service is absent from that list. The workaround is to use a top-level Decision as the BPMN entry point with informationRequirement edges to supporting decisions.

No -- Decision Service is NOT in Camunda 8's DMN element coverage (Pattern #35). md:23-28 lists exactly four supported DMN elements: Decision (tables and literal expressions), Input data, Knowledge source, Business knowledge model. Decision Service is absent. Canonical C8 substitute: expose the desired top-level Decision as the BPMN-side entry point (Business Rule Task references the Decision's decisionId); the DRD's informationRequirement edges propagate internal-Decision evaluations transitively without exposing them to the BPMN side. The BPMN Business Rule Task acts as the public surface; internal Decisions are evaluated automatically as DRD dependencies of the top-level Decision.

Camunda 8 automatically generates a Decision Service element from the DRD graph structure without any modelling effort -- no such automatic Decision Service generation feature exists in Camunda 8's DMN modeller or engine. Teams must explicitly architect their DRD with designated entry-point decisions and informationRequirement edges to supporting decisions. The engine evaluates the graph transitively but there is no automatic service abstraction layer created on top of the DRD.

BPMN Business Rule Tasks can reference any deployed Decision by decisionId, making each decision individually callable. The "public API" in Camunda 8 is implicit: whichever decisionId you reference in a Business Rule Task is the exposed entry point.

a.text: Incorrect. Per dmn.md:23-28 verbatim: "Modeler supports the following DMN elements: - Decision (tables and literal expressions) - Input data - Knowledge source - Business knowledge model" Decision Service is not in that list (Pattern #35 -- confirmed 5th cumulative across audits). The DMN-1.3 spec's Decision Service element (with outputDecision / encapsulatedDecision / inputDecision / inputData sub-elements) is not implemented in Camunda 8's modeler nor in the Zeebe DMN engine. Canonical C8 substitute pattern: 1. Author the "service entry point" as a top-level Decision (Decision Table or Literal Expression body). 2. Other supporting Decisions become graph dependencies via informationRequirement edges (DRD arrows from supporting Decision → entry-point Decision). 3. From BPMN, place a Business Rule Task that references the entry-point Decision's decisionId per business-rule-tasks.md:25-44. 4. The Zeebe DMN engine evaluates the entry-point Decision; the engine automatically pulls in graph dependencies -- supporting Decisions evaluate first, results propagate via the edges, entry-point Decision evaluates last and returns to the BPMN. This achieves the public-API encapsulation the spec's Decision Service offers, modulo: No formal outputDecision curation list -- the BPMN picks the entry point by decisionId; whichever Decision is referenced is "the public one." Other deployed Decisions remain callable on their own from any other BPMN unless you handle access control separately. No encapsulatedDecision curation list -- supporting Decisions are still individually deployable Decisions; they're "internal" only by convention. No formal inputDecision curation list -- inputs are the entry-point Decision's input data per the DRD. For stronger encapsulation in C8: deploy supporting decisions in a separate DMN file and don't reference them from any BPMN process -- they evaluate only as DRD dependencies. Resource-level Decision deletion/lifecycle per resource-deletion.md plus authorization on BPMN Business Rule Tasks is the closest C8 has to "public API" curation.

b.text: Correct. DMN-1.3 Decision Service element not in C8 modeler per dmn.md:23-28.

d.text: Incorrect. Each deployed Decision is callable from any BPMN by decisionId -- there's no service-level curation construct, so "publicly callable" is implicit (no enforcement). Authorization on BPMN side per business-rule-tasks.md is the gating mechanism.

explanation: DMN-1.3 Decision Service element not in C8 modeler per dmn.md:23-28.
